title: Pentagon requests $200B+ war supplemental from Congress
description: Pentagon asks White House to approve more than $200 billion supplemental budget request to Congress to fund Iran war — largest war funding ask since Iraq. Funds would urgently increase production of critical munitions expended in 3-week campaign.

title: Abu Dhabi suspends Habshan gas complex operations after missile debris
description: Gas operations at Abu Dhabi's Habshan gas complex and Bab oil field temporarily suspended after debris from intercepted Iranian missiles fell on facilities. UAE air defenses intercepted 13 ballistic missiles and 27 drones. No injuries reported.

title: IMO opens emergency session on Hormuz shipping crisis
description: UN maritime body opens extraordinary council session in London. 3,200 ships trapped west of Hormuz, 20,000 seafarers stranded, 7 merchant sailors killed. Council to vote Thursday on safe maritime corridor proposal backed by Bahrain, Japan, Panama, Singapore, UAE and US.

title: IDF destroys two Litani River bridges, isolating south Lebanon
description: Israeli military destroys two bridges over Litani River, cutting off southern Lebanon. Defense Minister Katz says bridges used by Hezbollah to smuggle weapons. Approximately 150,000 civilians remain trapped south of the river. IDF orders evacuation south of Zahrani River.

title: IRGC names 5 Gulf energy facilities as imminent targets
description: IRGC declares five Gulf energy facilities 'direct and legitimate targets' to be struck 'in the coming hours': Saudi Arabia's SAMREF refinery and Jubail petrochemical complex, UAE's Al Hosn gasfield, Qatar's Ras Laffan refinery and Mesaieed petrochemical complex. Warns workers to evacuate immediately. Saudi Aramco evacuates SAMREF personnel.

title: Iranian cluster missile kills elderly couple in Ramat Gan
description: Iranian cluster munition penetrates apartment roof in Ramat Gan, killing elderly couple Yaron and Ilana Moshe (70s). Foreign worker also killed at Moshav Adanim. Iran used Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr missiles. Shrapnel damages Tel Aviv train station. Israel death toll rises to 17.

title: Israel DM Katz warns of 'significant surprises' — authorizes targeting any Iranian official
description: Defense Minister Katz warns of 'significant surprises Wednesday across all arenas.' Announces Netanyahu authorized IDF to eliminate any senior Iranian official without additional approval. Says 'intensity of attacks in Iran is increasing — we are achieving a decisive outcome.'

title: Senate Defeats Third War Powers Resolution 53-47
description: Sen. Booker's resolution to halt Iran war defeated 53-47. Fetterman voted with GOP, Rand Paul with Dems. Democrats pledge weekly votes.

title: Trump Issues 60-Day Jones Act Waiver
description: Foreign-flagged vessels now allowed to transport oil, gas, fertilizer between US ports. Aimed at easing fuel prices. Analysts say impact ~3 cents/gallon.

title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates — Dow Drops 768 Points
description: Fed holds rates at 3.5-3.75%. Powell says oil disruption 'likely short-lived.' Dot plot signals only 1 cut this year. Dow drops 768 pts (-1.63%). PPI jumped 0.7% vs 0.3% expected.

title: Germany's Merz: 'Bombing Iran Will Not Achieve Regime Change'
description: Chancellor Merz shifts to open criticism of war. Warns of 'perpetual war,' refuses Hormuz involvement. Says NATO is 'defensive, not offensive.'

title: Riyadh Emergency FM Meeting — Missile Alert During Session
description: 10+ Arab/Islamic foreign ministers met in Riyadh (Turkey, Pakistan, Qatar, UAE, Egypt). No US present. Saudi capital received missile warning alerts during the meeting.

title: Ras Laffan Struck Second Time — LNG Fires Rage
description: Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan with ballistic missiles a second time in 12 hours. QatarEnergy: 'sizeable fires and extensive further damage' to multiple LNG facilities.

title: Trump: Israel Won't Strike South Pars Again, But US Will If Qatar Hit
description: Trump posted 'NO MORE ATTACKS' on South Pars but threatened US would 'massively blow up the entirety' of the field if Iran attacks Qatar again. Axios: Trump DID coordinate original strike with Netanyahu despite denying it.

title: Iran Fires 6+ Missile Salvos at Israel Overnight
description: IRGC fired at least 6 separate ballistic missile salvos at central and northern Israel using cluster bomb warheads. Most hit open areas. IRGC framing as sustained 'punishment' for assassinations.

title: Foreign Worker Killed at Moshav Adanim by Cluster Missile
description: 30-year-old foreign worker killed by shrapnel from Iranian cluster missile at Moshav Adanim. 4+ injured in related strikes on Bnei Brak, Kafr Qassem, Jaljulia.

title: Israeli Drones Kill 3 Lebanese Army Soldiers
description: Israeli drone strikes killed 3 uniformed Lebanese Armed Forces soldiers and injured 5 in Nabatieh, Zibdin, Doueir. Lebanon President Aoun condemned strikes. Israel says 'under review.'

title: Saudi Arabia: 'Trust in Iran Completely Shattered'
description: Riyadh FM Prince Faisal declared 'little trust that remained in Iran has been completely shattered.' Said Saudi Arabia 'reserves the right to take military action.'

title: FBI Investigating Joe Kent for Classified Leaks
description: FBI opened investigation into whether resigned NCTC Director Joe Kent leaked classified information when he publicly stated there was 'no intelligence' supporting an imminent Iranian threat.

title: Asian Markets Crash — Nikkei -2.7%, Brent Breaks $110
description: Asian markets fell sharply on March 19 open. Nikkei -2.7%, KOSPI -2.6%, Hang Seng -1.4%. Brent crude breached $110/barrel. Dubai crude hit all-time high above $150 for Asian buyers.

title: Israel Strikes South Pars Gas Field — 4 Gas Treatment Plants Taken Offline
description: Israeli Air Force, coordinated with the US, struck four gas treatment facilities at Iran's South Pars gas field in Bushehr Province and nearby Asaluyeh oil infrastructure. Facilities taken offline to contain fires. South Pars accounts for 36% of Iran's gas reserves.
location: South Pars / Asaluyeh, Bushehr Province, Iran

title: Israel Assassinates Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — 3rd Senior Official in 48 Hours
description: Israeli overnight airstrike killed Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib, the third senior Iranian official assassinated in 48 hours after Larijani and IRGC Aerospace chief. President Pezeshkian confirmed the death.

title: IDF First-Ever Caspian Sea Strike — Bandar Anzali Naval Base Destroyed
description: IDF Air Force struck Iranian Navy vessels at Bandar Anzali port on the Caspian Sea, destroying four missile boats, a corvette, and naval infrastructure. First IDF strikes in northern Iran, targeting the Iran-Russia supply lifeline at ~1,300 km from Israeli territory.

title: Iran Retaliates on ALL Gulf Energy Infrastructure — Ras Laffan, SAMREF, Habshan Hit
description: Iran attacked energy facilities across all six GCC countries: Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG complex (extensive damage, Shell Pearl GTL plant damaged), Saudi SAMREF refinery in Yanbu, two Kuwaiti gas units, UAE's Habshan gas facility. 2 killed in Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia.

title: Iran 'Revenge' Missile Strike Kills Elderly Couple in Ramat Gan
description: Iran launched revenge missile strikes on Israel after the Larijani assassination. A couple in their 70s killed in their Ramat Gan apartment when they could not reach their safe room in time. Several others injured.

title: Pentagon Requests $200 Billion War Supplemental — Largest Since Iraq/Afghanistan
description: Pentagon submitted $200 billion supplemental budget request to Congress for the Iran war, the largest military supplemental since Iraq/Afghanistan. Hegseth: 'It takes money to kill bad guys.' White House OMB initially objected to the total.

title: Senate Blocks War Powers Resolution 53-47 — Third Failed Vote
description: Senate Republicans blocked Sen. Cory Booker's war powers resolution 53-47. Rand Paul was the only Republican to vote for it; Fetterman the only Democrat to vote against. Third failed war powers vote since the war began.

title: Israeli Strikes Kill 11 Civilians in Western Iran — Dorud and Chegeni Hit
description: Israeli strikes in western Iran killed 7 in Dorud city and injured 56; another strike killed 4 in the Chegeni region. Multiple residential areas impacted.
location: Dorud and Chegeni, Lorestan Province, Iran

title: Qatar Expels Iran Military Attaches After Ras Laffan Attack
description: Qatar declared Iran's military and security attaches persona non grata, ordering them out within 24 hours after Iranian missile attacks caused extensive damage to Ras Laffan LNG complex and Shell's Pearl GTL plant.

title: DNI Gabbard Reveals Iran Nuclear Program 'Obliterated' — Not Rebuilding Before War
description: DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified Iran's nuclear enrichment was 'obliterated' by June 2025 strikes with 'no efforts to rebuild,' contradicting Trump's stated war rationale. Confirmed Mojtaba Khamenei 'severely injured,' status unclear.

title: Hegseth Announces 'Largest Strike Package Yet' — 7,000+ Targets Hit Total
description: Defense Secretary Hegseth announced the US would launch its largest strike package yet on Iran on March 19. US has struck 7,000+ targets. 64 Apache helicopters deployed against Iranian drones. 5,000-pound bunker busters used on hardened Hormuz missile sites.

title: EU Summit Refuses to Join US Military Campaign or Secure Hormuz
description: EU leaders in Brussels refused to join US/Israeli military campaign or help secure the Strait of Hormuz. Spanish PM Sanchez: 'We are against this war because it is illegal.' Austrian Chancellor Stocker: Europe 'will not allow itself to be blackmailed.'

title: US/Israeli Airstrikes Hit Iraq PMF Positions — 2 Killed in Salahuddin and Nineveh
description: Dawn airstrikes hit PMF 6th and 31st Brigades in Salahuddin Province (Beiji HQ) and PMF 4th Regiment in Nineveh Plains, killing 2 and injuring several. Islamic Resistance in Iraq has claimed 290+ attacks on US forces since Feb 28.

title: HIMARS Fires from Bahrain at Iran — First US Ballistic Missiles Launched from Gulf State
description: NBC verified video shows US-built HIMARS launchers firing missiles at Iran from Bahrain — first documented use of American ballistic missiles fired from a Gulf state in the conflict.

title: Israeli Bombardment Kills 33 Across Lebanon on March 19 — Litani Bridges Destroyed
description: Israeli strikes across Lebanon kill 33 and wound 152 on March 19 alone. At least 2 bridges over the Litani River destroyed. Strike on Bashoura in central Beirut. Total Lebanon toll: 1,001+ killed.

title: US F-35 Makes Emergency Landing After Suspected Iranian Fire — First Hostile Hit on F-35
description: A USAF F-35 Lightning II was struck by suspected Iranian fire during a combat mission over Iran and forced to make an emergency landing at a US base in the region. CENTCOM confirmed the aircraft 'landed safely.' Pilot is in stable condition. First confirmed hostile hit on an F-35 in history.

casualties.note: Pilot in stable condition, aircraft damaged

title: Iranian Missile Shrapnel Strikes Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery — Fires Extinguished
description: Shrapnel from an intercepted Iranian ballistic missile struck the Bazan Group oil refinery in Haifa, sparking fires that were quickly extinguished. No hazardous material leak. Israel Electric Corp reported a 45-minute power outage in Haifa area from a damaged power line. IDF clarified it was fragments from an intercept, not a direct hit.

casualties.note: No casualties at refinery; 4 wounded in Kiryat Shmona separately

title: Rockets Hit Kiryat Shmona — 4 Wounded Including 1 Serious
description: Hezbollah rockets struck Kiryat Shmona in northern Israel, with one projectile directly hitting a building. MDA treated a 60-year-old man (serious, shrapnel), a 68-year-old woman (moderate, head injury), and two men in their 20s (light shrapnel wounds).

casualties.note: 4 wounded (1 serious, 1 moderate, 2 light)

title: Iranian Cluster Munitions Kill 3 Palestinian Women in West Bank Salon
description: Three Palestinian women were killed in the southern West Bank when Iranian missile fragments struck a hair salon. A fourth woman was also reported killed. Separately, a 30-year-old foreign worker was killed at Moshav Adanim in central Israel by cluster bomb shrapnel.

casualties.note: 3-4 Palestinian women killed, 1 foreign worker killed

title: UNSC Adopts Resolution 2817 Condemning Iran's Attacks on Gulf States
description: UN Security Council adopted Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's 'egregious attacks' against Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Jordan. Passed 13-0 with 2 abstentions (China and Russia). First formal UN condemnation of Iran's attacks on Gulf neighbors.

title: Netanyahu Evening Press Conference — Claims Iran Can No Longer Enrich Uranium
description: Netanyahu held his second press conference, claiming Iran 'can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles.' Said Israel needs a 'ground component' for regime change. Confirmed Israel 'acted alone' on South Pars but is holding off per Trump's request. Said war will end 'a lot faster than people think.'

title: Gabbard Faces House Intelligence Committee — Dodges Nuclear Threat Question Again
description: DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified before the House Intelligence Committee alongside CIA Director Ratcliffe and FBI Director Patel. Democrats pressed: 'Did you deliver the assessment that there was no imminent nuclear threat?' Gabbard again declined to directly answer. This is a separate hearing from the March 18 Senate testimony.

title: Macron Calls Trump and Qatar Emir — Urges Moratorium on Civilian Infrastructure Strikes
description: French President Macron spoke with both the Emir of Qatar and President Trump, urging a 'moratorium on strikes targeting civilian infrastructure' especially water and energy facilities.

title: Six Western Allies Issue Joint Hormuz Statement — Ready to 'Ensure Safe Passage'
description: UK, France, Germany, Japan, and two additional Western allies issued a joint statement saying they are ready 'to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.' UK sent military planners to work with CENTCOM. ~20,000 seafarers on ~3,200 vessels remain stranded.

title: Hegseth announces 'largest strike package yet' against Iran
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the US will launch its 'largest strike package yet' against Iran. Said US has struck over 7,800 targets, destroyed all 11 Iranian subs, sunk or damaged 120+ warships, hit 40+ mine-laying vessels. Pentagon requesting $200B war supplemental from Congress. 'It takes money to kill bad guys.'

title: IDF strikes 200+ targets including first Bandar Anzali strike on Caspian coast
description: IDF struck over 200 targets in western and central Iran including missile storage, UAV facilities, air defense systems, and weapons production sites. First-ever strike on Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea coast — 5 Iranian Navy missile boats, a headquarters, and a shipyard destroyed. Joint Chiefs Chairman Caine said forces striking 'deeper' into Iran using 5,000-lb bunker busters.

title: Iran executes 3 men linked to January protests, including 19-year-old wrestler
description: Iran executed three men in Qom in connection with January 2026 nationwide anti-government protests, including a 19-year-old wrestler whose conviction drew US criticism. Rights groups described the trials as shams based on forced confessions and torture. First public hangings linked to the protest crackdown.

title: Qatar expels Iranian military and security personnel
description: Qatar declared Iranian military and security forces 'persona non grata,' giving them 24 hours to leave the country. Qatar called the Ras Laffan assault 'a dangerous escalation, a flagrant violation of its sovereignty.' Qatar had remained neutral since war began but said Iranian forces 'continuously targeted' it and neighbors.

title: Canada joins 7-nation Hormuz joint statement with UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan
description: Canada joined the joint statement on the Strait of Hormuz, expanding the coalition to 7 nations. Leaders expressed 'readiness to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage.' Called for immediate moratorium on attacks on civilian infrastructure including oil and gas installations. Marked shift from earlier European reluctance to join naval operations.

title: IMO to negotiate humanitarian corridor for 20,000 stranded seafarers in Gulf
description: The UN's International Maritime Organization announced it will begin negotiating with countries to establish a humanitarian corridor to free approximately 20,000 seafarers stranded in the Gulf by the war and Hormuz blockade.

title: State Department approves arms sales to Gulf countries
description: The US State Department approved new arms sales to Gulf countries amid ongoing Iranian attacks on their energy infrastructure and territory. Move comes as Gulf states face continued Iranian missile and drone strikes on oil, gas, and refinery facilities.

title: IAF choosing not to intercept some Iranian cluster bomblets to conserve interceptors
description: Israeli Air Force has begun making tactical decisions not to shoot down some Iranian cluster submunitions to conserve interceptor missiles. About half of Iranian missiles since Feb 28 carry cluster warheads with ~24 submunitions each, breaking apart at 7-10 km altitude. Overall interception rate for missiles targeting populated areas: 90%.

title: Joe Kent under FBI investigation for classified Iran war leaks
description: Former National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent is under FBI investigation for alleged leaks of classified information related to the Iran war. Kent resigned earlier this week over the Trump administration's handling of the conflict.

title: Bessent: US may unsanction 140M barrels of Iranian oil on tankers to lower prices
description: Treasury Secretary Bessent announced the US may remove sanctions on ~140M barrels of Iranian oil stranded on tankers worldwide, flooding the market to reduce prices for 10-14 days. 'Using the Iranian barrels against the Iranians.' Also flagged potential additional SPR release beyond IEA's 400M barrel emergency.

title: Airstrikes hit Iraq PMF brigades in Salahuddin and Nineveh — 3 killed
description: Airstrikes (attributed to US/Israel) hit Iraq's PMF 6th and 31st Brigades in Salahuddin province (Beiji HQ and Al Siniya checkpoint), killing 2 fighters. A third strike hit the PMF 4th Regiment of the 30th Brigade in Nineveh Plains, killing 1 and injuring 2.

title: IDF kills 20+ Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon — Golani Brigade
description: IDF killed 20+ Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon over 24 hours. Golani Brigade engaged fighters launching anti-tank missiles. IDF issued evacuation order south of Zahrani River. Weapons confiscated including RPGs and anti-tank rockets.

title: IRGC Spokesperson Gen. Naeini killed in Israeli airstrike
description: IDF killed IRGC spokesperson Brig. Gen. Ali Mohammad Naeini in overnight airstrike targeting command centers in Tehran/Karaj region. He had hours earlier claimed Iran was still producing missiles. 4th senior Iranian official killed this week after Larijani, Soleimani, and Khatib.

title: Israel strikes Tehran on Nowruz — wave of attacks on Persian New Year
description: Israel pounded Tehran with airstrikes Friday morning as Iranians marked Nowruz. IDF announced 'wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime.' Multiple explosions reported across the capital.

title: Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery hit again by Iranian drones
description: Two waves of Iranian drone strikes hit Kuwait's largest refinery (730K bpd) for second consecutive day, sparking fires. Several units shut down as precaution. No casualties reported.

title: Saudi SAMREF refinery at Yanbu hit by Iranian drone
description: Drone struck SAMREF refinery (Saudi Aramco/ExxonMobil JV, 400K bpd) in Red Sea port of Yanbu. Saudi air defenses also intercepted ballistic missile targeting Yanbu Port and drones over Riyadh and Eastern Province.

title: Dubai explosions — UAE air defenses intercept Iranian fire on Eid
description: Heavy explosions shook Dubai as UAE air defenses intercepted incoming Iranian fire during Eid al-Fitr celebrations. Dubai Media Office confirmed explosions were from successful interceptions.

title: Bahrain warehouse fire from intercepted Iranian projectile shrapnel
description: Bahrain Interior Ministry reported warehouse fire from shrapnel of intercepted Iranian projectile. Civil defense extinguished blaze, no injuries. Bahrain total interceptions since war start: 139 missiles, 238 drones.

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts missile over Al Jouf region
description: Saudi air defenses intercepted an Iranian missile over the northern Al Jouf region on Day 21.

title: USS Boxer ARG + 11th MEU departs San Diego for Middle East
description: USS Boxer Amphibious Ready Group (Boxer, Portland, Comstock) with 11th MEU (~4,000 personnel with F-35s) departed San Diego ahead of schedule. Joins ~50,000 US troops in region. Second MEU deployment to theater.

title: Netanyahu agrees to halt strikes on Iran energy sites after Trump demand
description: Netanyahu said Israel will comply with Trump's demand to stop attacking Iran energy infrastructure after South Pars gas field strike. Netanyahu: 'Israel acted alone. President Trump asked us to hold off.' Trump: 'I told him, don't do that.'

title: Pentagon requests $200B supplemental war budget from Congress
description: Pentagon asked White House to approve $200+ billion supplemental request to Congress for Iran war. Hegseth: 'That number could move. It takes money to kill bad guys.' First 100 hours cost $3.7B; first 12 days cost $16.5B per CSIS.

title: European Council calls for moratorium on energy/water facility strikes
description: All 27 EU heads of state issued conclusions calling for de-escalation, moratorium on strikes against energy and water facilities, and reopening of Strait of Hormuz. Condemned Iran's indiscriminate strikes on Gulf states.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei issues Nowruz statement — still not seen in public
description: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued Nowruz statement calling for enemies' 'security to be taken away.' Message read by TV anchor with still photo — Mojtaba has still not appeared in person since becoming Supreme Leader. US officials suggest he may have been injured.

title: Araghchi rejects ceasefire — 'negotiations off the table'
description: Iran FM Abbas Araghchi rejected ceasefire: 'We are not seeking a cease-fire because we do not want this scenario to be repeated again.' Cited bitter experience of US attacking during negotiations. On nuclear material: 'under the rubbles' with 'no plan to recover.'

title: Gabbard testimony: Iran NOT rebuilding enrichment before war
description: DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified Iran's regime is 'intact but largely degraded.' Intel community assessed Iran was NOT rebuilding enrichment capabilities before war — undercutting Trump's key justification. Joe Kent resigned from NCTC, saying Iran 'posed no imminent threat.'

title: IDF launches Nowruz strikes on Tehran — 'heart of regime'
description: The IDF announced it had 'begun a wave of strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian terror regime in the heart of Tehran' on Nowruz morning. Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei had not been publicly seen; Al Jazeera's correspondent described Tehran as 'hushed' with none of the customary Persian New Year festivities.

title: IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini killed in Nowruz strikes
description: Iran's IRGC confirmed Brigadier General Ali Mohammad Naini, its spokesman since 2024, was killed in an Israeli-American strike 'at dawn.' Naini had just stated hours before that Iran's 'missile industry score is 20' and that production was unaffected by the war. His death marks the fourth senior Iranian official killed by Israel this week.

title: Three more IRGC senior commanders eliminated on Nowruz eve
description: Israel eliminated three additional IRGC commanders on the eve of Nowruz: Esmail Ahmadi-Moghaddam (Basij intelligence chief), Mehdi Gharish (aerospace forces commander), and Kavous Dehrood (special forces commander). Combined with Naini and Khatib earlier, Iran has lost five senior officials in two days.

title: Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery struck again by Iranian drones
description: Iranian drones hit Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery complex (capacity 730,000 bpd) again on Day 21, sparking fires as crews worked to contain the blaze. The refinery had been targeted previously. Kuwait's PM summoned Iran's ambassador and the country has now activated emergency response protocols.

title: Bessent: US to lift sanctions on 140M barrels of Iranian crude
description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced the US may lift sanctions on approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian crude stranded on tankers at sea. 'In essence, we will be using the Iranian barrels against the Iranians to keep the price down,' Bessent said, explaining that the oil would flow to Asia instead of China. Brent crude rose 1.3% to $110.28 on the news.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei makes first public statement as Supreme Leader
description: New Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, 56, issued his first public statement since succeeding his father, saying 'Iran's enemies must have their security taken away.' The statement came in response to Israel's killing of intelligence chief Esmail Khatib. Khamenei had not been seen publicly since the strikes began.

title: Lebanon death toll surpasses 1,001
description: Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed that 1,001 people have been killed in Lebanon since Israel renewed widespread attacks on the country, with more than 2,584 wounded. Among the dead are at least 118 children. More than one million Lebanese nationals — one-sixth of the population — have been displaced. President Aoun again called for a truce.

title: Trump calls NATO allies 'COWARDS' over Strait of Hormuz — 'NATO IS A PAPER TIGER'
description: President Trump posted on Truth Social calling NATO allies 'COWARDS' for not helping open the Strait of Hormuz: 'Now that fight is Militarily WON, with very little danger for them, they complain about the high oil prices they are forced to pay, but don't want to help open the Strait of Hormuz, a simple military maneuver that is the single reason for the high oil prices. So easy for them to do, with so little risk. COWARDS, and we will REMEMBER!' Trump added: 'Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!' The post came in response to six Western allies offering limited post-ceasefire Hormuz support.

title: IDF claims air supremacy over most of Iran — 70% missile launchers destroyed, 3+ more weeks planned
description: The IDF announced it has achieved air supremacy over most of Iran's airspace, having destroyed more than 100 air defense systems and approximately 120 detection systems. The military claims to have destroyed or disabled approximately 70% of Iran's estimated 500 ballistic missile launchers. IDF Brig. Gen. Defrin stated the IDF is planning at least three more weeks of operations to systematically degrade Iran's defense industry. The announcement came on Nowruz, the Persian New Year, as IDF strikes continued in Tehran.

title: Iran's 8th missile salvo since midnight — fragments land 400m from Western Wall in Jerusalem
description: Iran launched its eighth ballistic missile attack on Israel since midnight on Day 21. Sirens sounded in the southern Negev and northern Israel. An intercepted missile left fragments in the Jewish Quarter of Jerusalem's Old City, approximately 400 meters from the Western Wall — the holiest accessible Jewish site. A separate missile impact was reported in Rehovot. No Israeli casualties reported for this specific wave. The strikes coincided with Nowruz, the Persian New Year, and Eid al-Fitr.

title: IDF kills Hezbollah intelligence chief Hussein Makled in Beirut overnight strikes
description: The IDF announced it killed Hussein Makled, described as the head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters, in overnight airstrikes on Beirut. His elimination was accompanied by new Israeli evacuation orders for villages in southern Lebanon ahead of anticipated expanded ground operations. Lebanon's Health Ministry confirmed the overall death toll had surpassed 1,001 killed, with more than 1,049,328 people displaced — approximately one in five Lebanese. The UN warned nearly a fifth of Lebanon's population was uprooted.

title: Iran fires ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia — unprecedented 4,000km range
description: Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles toward Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK military base in the British Indian Ocean Territory — the longest-range Iranian strike ever attempted at approximately 4,000 km, double Iran's previously claimed 2,000 km range. The attack came hours after UK PM Starmer gave the US permission to use Diego Garcia to target Iranian missile sites. One missile reportedly failed mid-flight; a US destroyer fired an SM-3 interceptor at the second. Neither missile struck the base. The attack demonstrates a previously unknown Iranian IRBM capability and opens a new geographic front far from the Persian Gulf.
location: Diego Garcia, British Indian Ocean Territory

casualties.note: Base not hit; both missiles failed to impact

title: Trump signals 'winding down' of Iran war — calls NATO 'COWARDS' on Truth Social
description: President Trump signaled the US may be approaching an end to Iran war operations, posting on Truth Social that the US is 'getting very close to meeting our objectives as we consider winding down our great Military efforts.' In a speech to Naval Academy midshipmen, Trump stated 'their leaders are all gone... there's nobody to talk to' in Iran. Trump separately blasted NATO allies as 'COWARDS' for refusing to commit forces to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, writing 'Without the U.S.A., NATO IS A PAPER TIGER!' Trump also ruled out ground troops, saying 'I'm not putting troops anywhere — if I were, I certainly wouldn't tell you.'

title: Iran develops Hormuz 'vetting system' — selective passage for friendly nations
description: Iran is developing a vetting system for selective transit through the Strait of Hormuz, allowing friendly nations to negotiate case-by-case passage. India, China, Turkey, Pakistan, and Malaysia are among nations in discussions. At least 9 ships have transited via an Iranian territorial waters corridor, with at least one tanker reportedly paying $2 million for passage rights. Around 8 non-Iranian vessels were detected via AIS on March 18 — nearly double recent days — signaling Iran may be partially softening the blockade for strategic partners while maintaining effective closure for Western shipping.

title: Iran fires cluster missiles at central Israel on Day 22 — Rishon Lezion daycare hit
description: Iran fired ballistic missiles with cluster warheads at central Israel on Day 22 (March 21). A cluster munition struck an empty daycare in Rishon Lezion — no casualties. Separately, missile debris from an intercepted Iranian ballistic missile landed in Jerusalem's Old City near the Temple Mount and Al-Aqsa Mosque, damaging a road and wall in the Jewish Quarter. The IDF said it identified three rounds of missile fire in the hour and a half preceding midnight and another barrage hours later.

title: UK authorizes US use of Diego Garcia for Hormuz strikes — Iran threatens UK bases
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer authorized the US to use Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK base in the British Indian Ocean Territory, for strikes against Iranian missile sites threatening the Strait of Hormuz. The authorization came hours before Iran launched its IRBM barrage at Diego Garcia (see evt-new-281). Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi warned that UK bases would be 'legitimate targets' following the authorization.
location: Diego Garcia, British Indian Ocean Territory

title: Natanz nuclear facility struck — US-Israel conduct major raid on Day 22
description: US and Israeli forces conducted a major airstrike on Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility on Day 22. Iranian state media agency Tasnim reported 'no radioactive leakage' from the site. The strike is the latest in a series of attacks on Iran's nuclear infrastructure, following earlier strikes on Fordow and Isfahan nuclear sites. The Natanz facility was Iran's primary centrifuge enrichment complex.

title: Araghchi: Iran seeks to 'end the war permanently' — sets conditions
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Iran 'seeks to end the war permanently,' according to a report by Al Arabiya. However, Araghchi also set conditions: a 'decisive and final end' to attacks plus war reparations. He dismissed US negotiations: 'There is no good experience in talking with the Americans.' The statement comes amid reports of a backchannel CIA-Iranian intelligence contact through a third country to discuss terms.

title: IDF destroys Iranian corvette and 4 missile boats at Bandar Anzali — Caspian naval base
description: Israeli forces conducted a rare strike on Iran's Caspian Sea coast, destroying a corvette, four missile boats, and naval infrastructure at the Bandar Anzali naval base. A shipyard and a command center were also struck. The strike extends the conflict to Iran's northern coast for the first time. CENTCOM separately confirmed Iran's naval forces have been effectively neutralized, stating 'their Navy is not sailing.'

title: Saudi Arabia intercepts 51 drones over Eastern Province — largest single Saudi interception
description: Saudi Arabia's Defense Ministry confirmed it intercepted and destroyed 51 Iranian drones over the Eastern Province on March 21 — coinciding with Nowruz and Eid al-Fitr. This is the largest single Saudi interception event of the conflict. The Eastern Province hosts Saudi Aramco's key infrastructure including the Ras Tanura refinery and Abqaiq processing facility. No casualties or infrastructure damage reported.

title: CENTCOM: Iran's military at 10% capacity — missile rate down 90%, navy and air force grounded
description: US Admiral Brad Cooper (CENTCOM) declared Iran's military capabilities have been reduced to approximately 10% of pre-war levels. Statement: 'Their Navy is not sailing, their tactical fighters are not flying and they have lost the ability to launch missiles and drones at the high rates seen at the beginning of the conflict.' Iran's missile launch rate is down 90% and drone rate down 83%. The US also dropped multiple 5,000-pound bombs on an underground coastal weapons storage facility to further degrade Hormuz-blocking capability. US-Israel campaign bombed 77% of identified tunnel entrances at 27 Iranian underground bases.
location: CENTCOM HQ, Abu Dhabi / Persian Gulf

title: 22-nation joint statement condemns Hormuz blockade — demands Iran restore shipping
description: Twenty-two countries issued a joint statement condemning Iran's attacks on commercial vessels and the 'de facto closure' of the Strait of Hormuz. Signatories: UAE, UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada, South Korea, New Zealand, Denmark, Latvia, Slovenia, Estonia, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Czechia, Romania, Bahrain, Lithuania, and Australia. Called for 'an immediate comprehensive moratorium on attacks on civilian infrastructure.' Notably absent: the US and China. Iran dismissed the statement.

title: Baghdad: Drone strike sets fire near US military complex — Iraqi militia attack
description: Iran-aligned Iraqi Shia militias launched a drone strike that ignited a fire near a US military complex in Baghdad. This is a repeat attack pattern — the complex has been targeted multiple times since the war began. No US casualties reported.

title: Israel Defense Minister Katz: attacks will increase significantly this week
description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz issued a statement declaring that the intensity of US-Israel attacks on Iran will "increase significantly" this week. "This week, the intensity of the attacks that the IDF and the U.S. military will carry out against the Iranian terrorist regime and against the infrastructures on which it relies will increase significantly." The announcement directly contradicted Trump's "winding down" rhetoric from the previous evening.

title: Pezeshkian Nowruz address: proposes "Islamic Assembly of Middle East" — rejects foreign interference
description: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian delivered a Nowruz (Persian New Year) address on March 21, 2026, calling for creation of an "Islamic Assembly of the Middle East" as a regional security structure. "We do not need the foreigners presence in the region. We can set up an Islamic assembly in the Middle East with the cooperation of Muslim states, and within that framework, regulate our security, economic, cultural and political relations." He also rejected Israel's narrative blaming Iran for regional instability, citing Israel as the true cause of turmoil. This is Iran's first major diplomatic peace proposal since the war began.

title: IDF releases footage of 5 simultaneous strikes on ballistic missile storage array in western Iran
description: The IDF released footage showing five near-simultaneous airstrikes on a large-scale ballistic missile storage array facility in western Iran. The strikes killed a "large number" of IRGC ballistic missile personnel. Overnight attacks also hit a central IRGC compound for ballistic missile component production, a missile production storage facility, and a Ministry of Defense compound producing missile fuel. The IDF stated these strikes "significantly degrade" Iran's ballistic missile production capability.

casualties.note: Large number of IRGC missile personnel killed per IDF

title: IAEA confirms no radiation leakage from Natanz 2nd airstrike — IDF denies involvement
description: The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) confirmed that its monitoring equipment showed no increase in off-site radiation levels following the second major airstrike on the Natanz uranium enrichment facility. Iranian state media and the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran confirmed the attack, stating nuclear material was "safe." The IDF denied knowledge of an Israeli role in the Natanz strike, suggesting the attack was US-only using bunker-buster munitions. Independent arms control experts warned the strikes may have only delayed Iran's nuclear capability by months, not years, if enriched uranium was moved before the strikes.

title: Iranian IRBM strikes Arad — 74 injured, air defense fails to intercept
description: An Iranian intermediate-range ballistic missile carrying a warhead 'weighing hundreds of kilograms' struck the center of Arad, a city of 30,000 in southern Israel, landing between apartment buildings. Israeli air defense systems were activated but failed to intercept the missile — the IDF opened a formal investigation into the failure. IDF Spokesperson Effie Defrin confirmed: 'The air defense systems were activated but did not intercept the missile. We will investigate the incident and learn from it.' Four buildings sustained extensive damage. MDA raised the injured toll to 74 (7 serious, 15 moderate, 52 light), the highest single-attack casualty count of the war.

casualties.note: 74 injured (7 serious, 15 moderate, 52 light) — highest single-attack casualty count of the war. Interception failure under IDF probe.

title: Iranian ballistic missile hits Dimona — near nuclear research center, 47+ injured
description: A separate Iranian ballistic missile struck Dimona, a city in the Negev desert, destroying a one-story building. The Negev Nuclear Research Center is located just outside the city — this was the first time Iran targeted the Dimona nuclear area in this war. IAEA stated it received 'no indication of damage to the nuclear research center Negev.' IDF said air defense was activated. Netanyahu called it 'a very difficult evening.' Among the injured: a 10-year-old boy in serious condition and a woman moderately wounded. Missile debris landed in multiple residential areas. Total injuries: 47 including 20 lightly wounded.

casualties.note: 47 injured including 10-year-old boy (serious). First Iranian strike in proximity to Dimona nuclear research center. IAEA confirmed no nuclear damage.

title: IDF strikes Malek Ashtar University nuclear weapons R&D facility in Tehran
description: The Israeli Air Force struck the Malek Ashtar University of Technology in Tehran, which the IDF described as a facility used to develop components for nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles. The IDF called the site 'strategic' and noted it had been under Western sanctions for decades. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir said damage was 'extensive.' The IDF also struck Iranian air defense systems in the Tehran area during the same operation. The university, part of Iran's defense industrial base, had been a key site for nuclear weapons component development under IRGC supervision.

title: US-Israeli airstrike kills child at Ahvaz entertainment complex — hospital struck in Andimeshk
description: A US-Israeli airstrike on the Ritaj entertainment complex in Ahvaz, Khuzestan Province, killed one child and injured both parents. Separately, a strike damaged the Imam Ali Hospital in Andimeshk, also in Khuzestan Province near the Iraq border — the hospital announced it was no longer accepting patients due to structural damage. An unexploded projectile also struck a residential building in Aghajari with no reported casualties. Khuzestan Province's deputy governor confirmed the casualties.

casualties.note: 1 child killed, 2 adults injured in Ahvaz. Hospital in Andimeshk damaged and closed to new patients.

title: Houthis threaten to close Bab el-Mandeb Strait — second global chokepoint threatened
description: Houthi political bureau member Mohammed al-Bukhaiti and military official Abed al-Thawr announced the group is actively considering closing the Bab el-Mandeb Strait — the Red Sea chokepoint connecting the Gulf of Aden to the Suez Canal route. The Houthis declared military alignment with Iran and 'Hour Zero' readiness. Any closure would initially target US and Israeli-linked ships. Shipping giants Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, and CMA CGM have already paused Trans-Suez sailings. A Bab el-Mandeb closure would create a 'double chokepoint' scenario alongside the already-closed Strait of Hormuz, potentially cutting off the Mediterranean and Suez route as well.

title: Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum: Open Hormuz or face strikes on Iran's power plants
description: President Trump posted an ultimatum on Truth Social at 7:44 PM ET: 'If Iran doesn't FULLY OPEN, WITHOUT THREAT, the Strait of Hormuz, within 48 HOURS from this exact point in time, the United States of America will hit and obliterate their various POWER PLANTS, STARTING WITH THE BIGGEST ONE FIRST!' The deadline expires approximately 7:44 PM ET Monday, March 23. Iran's military immediately issued a counter-threat, warning that if Iran's energy infrastructure is struck, 'all energy, information technology and desalination infrastructure belonging to the US and the regime in the region will be targeted.' This represented a major new escalation tier — the first explicit US threat to strike Iranian civilian power infrastructure.

title: IDF Chief Zamir: Iran campaign at 'halfway stage' — 3 more weeks of operations planned
description: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir stated the military campaign against Iran is at its 'halfway stage' with plans for at least 3 more weeks of operations. IDF has destroyed approximately 70% of Iran's estimated 500 ballistic missile launchers and 80% of Iran's air defense systems. CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper stated the US has struck over 8,000 targets in Iran, including 130 Iranian vessels — 'the largest elimination of a navy in a three-week period since World War II.' Zamir also noted that Iran's IRBM strike on Diego Garcia shows 'Berlin, Paris, and Rome are all within range' of Iranian missiles. The statement directly contradicted Trump's signals of winding down the war, highlighting US-Israel tensions over war termination.

title: Iran military counter-threatens to strike all US energy, IT, and desalination infrastructure in region
description: In response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum threatening Iran's power plants, the Iranian military issued a formal counter-threat via Fars News Agency: 'All energy, information technology, and desalination infrastructure belonging to the US and the regime in the region will be targeted' if Iran's energy grid is struck. The warning covers US military and allied energy assets across the Gulf, including desalination facilities critical to Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, and Qatar. The counter-threat raised fears of attacks on civilian water infrastructure and was described by analysts as an existential threat to Gulf states entirely dependent on desalination for fresh water.

title: USS Boxer + 11th MEU deploy to Middle East — US ground force option emerges
description: USS Boxer (LHD-4), an amphibious assault ship, departed San Diego with the 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) carrying approximately 2,200 Marines. A second MEU is also deploying, bringing total additional Marines to ~5,000 plus thousands of sailors. The US now has more than 50,000 troops in the Middle East. Pentagon officials confirmed no decision has been made to send troops into Iran, but the deployment builds ground-assault capacity. Military analysts noted USS Boxer's amphibious capability provides a coastal landing option, increasing pressure on Tehran.

title: Israel strikes Malek Ashtar University R&D complex in East Tehran
description: Israel launched strikes on East Tehran on Day 23, specifically targeting a research and development facility at Malek Ashtar University of Technology. The IDF announced the facility was used to develop components for nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles. Explosions were reported in eastern Tehran neighborhoods. The IDF stated: 'Israel will not allow the Iranian regime to acquire nuclear weapons.' The Malek Ashtar complex is Iran's leading defense engineering university and has been linked to IRGC missile and weapons programs.

title: Netanyahu at Arad: 'Europe must join the fight against Iran'
description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu visited Arad — the city struck by Iranian ballistic missiles the previous night — and urged European leaders to join the coalition against Iran. Netanyahu cited Iran's demonstrated ~4,000km IRBM range (evidenced by the Diego Garcia attack) as proof that Berlin, Paris, and Rome are within Iranian missile range. He stated: 'If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have proved it. They have the capacity to reach deep into Europe. It's time to see the leaders of the rest of the countries join up.' He called the Hormuz blockade 'blackmail of the entire world.'

title: Hezbollah fires 6 rockets at Metula; Lebanon death toll reaches 1,001
description: Hezbollah launched six rockets at Metula in northern Israel from southern Lebanon on Day 23. Three rockets crashed in Lebanese territory; three were intercepted by Israeli air defenses. Lebanon's Ministry of Public Health simultaneously confirmed that 1,001 people have been killed in Lebanon since the conflict expanded on March 2, with over 2,650 wounded and more than 1 million displaced. Israeli Defense Minister Katz ordered IDF strikes on all Litani River bridges to cut Hezbollah supply and movement routes southward.

title: Iran military: Hormuz 'completely closed indefinitely' if power plants struck
description: Iran's military operational command issued a formal counter-threat in response to Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to open the Strait of Hormuz: if Iran's power plants are struck, the strait will be 'completely closed indefinitely.' The statement also warned that all US and Israeli energy, information technology, and desalination infrastructure across the Gulf region would be targeted. With the Trump deadline expiring Monday March 23 at approximately 7:44 PM ET, Iran signaled it would not comply with the Hormuz reopening demand and would escalate significantly if its energy infrastructure is attacked.

title: Hezbollah rocket kills Israeli civilian Ofer Moskovitz at Misgav Am
description: Hezbollah fired rockets at Misgav Am in northern Israel on March 22, killing Ofer Moskovitz, 60 years old. The IDF opened an investigation into whether the death was caused by a Hezbollah projectile or Israeli artillery fire, as no warning sirens had sounded before the strike. Hezbollah claimed responsibility for 'a rocket barrage targeting a gathering of Israeli enemy army soldiers' in the area. The death raised the Israeli civilian death toll from Hezbollah to at least 3 since the conflict expansion began on March 2. Israel Defense Minister Katz ordered the IDF to immediately destroy all bridges over the Litani River used for Hezbollah supply and movement routes.

casualties.note: Ofer Moskovitz, 60, killed by Hezbollah rocket at Misgav Am

title: Iran fires 8th missile salvo of the day at Israel — 150+ wounded across multiple cities
description: Iran's IRGC fired an eighth wave of ballistic missiles at Israel on March 22, targeting Arad, Dimona, Eilat, Beersheba, and Kiryat Gat. The combined day's salvos wounded approximately 150-180 people, with at least 11 in serious condition. Israeli emergency services reported Israeli air defenses intercepted the majority of incoming missiles, but at least two direct hits caused structural damage in Arad. The IRGC stated the attacks were in retaliation for US-Israeli strikes on Iran's Natanz nuclear facility the previous day. Iran confirmed zero deaths on the Israeli side from its own count, contrary to Iranian state media claims. Israeli Defense Ministry confirmed the salvos and said the multi-front attack from Iran and Hezbollah simultaneously was being analyzed.

casualties.note: 150-180 wounded across southern Israel; 11 serious. No confirmed deaths from Day 23 salvos.

title: IRGC releases 'Say goodbye to electricity' map of Gulf energy targets in response to Trump ultimatum
description: Iran's IRGC released an official targeting map via Mehr News Agency depicting power plants, energy infrastructure, and desalination facilities across UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, and Bahrain captioned 'Say goodbye to electricity.' The map was issued as a direct counter-threat following President Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to open the Strait of Hormuz or face US strikes on Iranian power plants. Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf stated that vital energy and oil infrastructure across the region would become legitimate targets if Iran's own power infrastructure is struck. The threat was directed at US-allied Gulf states hosting US military assets.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iranian authorities taunt US/Israel/EU

title: Iran grants Japan safe passage through Hormuz; developing IRGC vetting system for transit
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi announced on March 21 that Japan-related ships are approved to transit the Strait of Hormuz, joining China, India, and Pakistan as nations granted selective passage. Iran is simultaneously developing a formal IRGC registration and vetting system for Hormuz transit, requiring vessels to pre-submit ownership documentation, cargo details, and destination information to IRGC naval command. Reports indicate at least one tanker paid approximately $2 million for transit rights. The dual-track approach — selective approval for friendly nations, vetting for others — represents Iran's attempt to maintain leverage over global oil supply while avoiding total isolation. Some 40,000 seafarers remain stranded on ships on either side of the strait.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Iran allows Japanese ships

title: EU announces new sanctions on 19 Iranian officials for human rights violations
description: EU High Representative Kaja Kallas announced on March 22 that EU Member States ambassadors approved new sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities responsible for serious human rights violations during the conflict. The sanctions complement existing EU restrictive measures and were coordinated with calls to keep the Strait of Hormuz open. Kallas also held calls with Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea on conflict de-escalation. The announcement came as Europe publicly declined to send NATO warships to Hormuz but increased diplomatic pressure on Iran.

title: US drops GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator bombs on Iranian anti-ship missile bunkers near Strait of Hormuz
description: CENTCOM announced US forces deployed multiple GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator (5,000-pound deep-penetrating bunker buster) bombs against hardened Iranian anti-ship missile sites along the coastline overlooking the Strait of Hormuz. CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper stated the strikes destroyed underground hardened facilities housing Iranian anti-ship cruise missiles, mobile missile launchers, intelligence support sites, and ship-tracking radar relays used to monitor vessel movements in the strait. Cooper reported US forces have now struck over 8,000 Iranian targets total, including the destruction of 130 vessels — describing it as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' The GBU-72 is designed to penetrate deeply hardened underground facilities that smaller munitions cannot destroy.
location: Iranian coastline near Strait of Hormuz

sources.name: Gulf News — CENTCOM GBU-72 Hormuz strike
Maritime Executive — US hits Iran antiship missile sites
The Hill — 5,000-lb deep-penetrator bombs near Hormuz

title: Netanyahu visits Arad missile strike site; calls on world leaders to join war against Iran
description: Prime Minister Netanyahu visited the city of Arad on March 22, where Iranian ballistic missiles struck residential buildings overnight, killing no one but wounding 115+ residents including children. At the site, Netanyahu stated: 'If you want proof that Iran endangers the entire world, the last 48 hours have given it.' He cited three events: Iran targeted civilians in Arad; Iran 'fired on Jerusalem, right next to the holy sites of the three monotheistic faiths'; and Iran 'fired an intercontinental ballistic missile on Diego Garcia.' Netanyahu called on world leaders to formally join the US-Israel coalition: 'It's time for world leaders to join us, and I'm seeing movement from some in that direction.' Separately, IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir declared the fight with Hezbollah is 'only just begun' and the war will continue 'several more weeks.' Ben Gurion Airport imposed flight restrictions effective Monday afternoon.

title: Iranian cluster munition ballistic missiles scatter submunitions across central Israel; 15 wounded in Tel Aviv
description: Cluster bomb fragments from Iranian ballistic missiles landed across at least 7 sites in central Israel on March 22, including the Tel Aviv metropolitan area, wounding 15 people. The cluster munitions were delivered via ballistic missiles and scattered submunitions over residential neighborhoods. Iran's use of cluster munitions — internationally condemned weapons that scatter indiscriminate bomblets over wide areas — marked a new phase of Iranian targeting tactics. The International Committee of the Red Cross and human rights organizations condemned the use of such weapons against civilian-populated areas. Separately, on March 23, additional cluster missile impacts were reported in central Israel with no new injuries. Israel's military confirmed cluster warhead fragments were found at multiple sites.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — cluster missile Tel Aviv
Defence Security Asia — Iranian cluster munitions OSINT

title: IDF overnight strikes destroy 4 IRGC missile production and fuel facilities in Tehran; power outages hit western Tehran and Karaj
description: In overnight strikes following Day 23, Israeli forces struck four major Tehran targets: a central IRGC compound used for missile component production and development; a storage site for missile production parts; a Defense Ministry facility responsible for ballistic missile fuel production; and a second ballistic missile component production site. Israel said the operation 'significantly damaged Iran's ability to keep producing key ballistic missile components.' Power substation explosions followed in western Tehran and Karaj, causing temporary electricity outages across both cities. Iran state media confirmed the strikes but downplayed damage.

sources.name: Iran International — IDF Tehran overnight strikes
VINnews — IDF strikes Iranian weapons sites in Tehran

title: Israel orders destruction of all Litani River bridges used by Hezbollah; Qasmiyeh coastal bridge destroyed
description: Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz ordered the military to destroy all bridges over Lebanon's Litani River that Hezbollah uses for 'terrorist activity,' and to accelerate demolition of Lebanese homes in front-line villages near the Israeli border. Israeli aircraft struck and destroyed the Qasmiyeh Bridge — a major coastal artery linking Sidon with Tyre and southern Lebanon. Lebanese President Joseph Aoun warned the strikes are 'a prelude to a ground invasion' and condemned them as collective punishment. The IDF said the operation aims to sever Hezbollah's supply and logistics routes. Over 1,024 Lebanese have been killed since fighting began on March 2.

sources.name: US News/AP — Israel orders Litani bridges destroyed
The National — Israel threatens Gaza-scale destruction in Lebanon
Al Jazeera — Israeli strike hits Qasmiyeh bridge

title: CENTCOM: US has struck 8,000+ Iranian military targets; 130 Iranian naval vessels destroyed — largest navy elimination since WWII
description: Admiral Brad Cooper, commander of US Central Command, announced that the United States has struck over 8,000 Iranian military targets since the start of the war — a major operational milestone. He described the destruction of 130 Iranian naval vessels as 'the largest elimination of a navy over a three-week period since World War II.' CENTCOM also confirmed it dropped multiple 5,000-pound GBU-72 Advanced Penetrator bombs on an underground coastal facility Iran used to store anti-ship cruise missiles and mobile missile launchers along the Strait of Hormuz, specifically to neutralize Iran's threat to Gulf shipping.

sources.name: CBS News live updates — CENTCOM 8,000 targets

title: IDF kills Hezbollah Radwan Force special operations commander Abu Khalil Barji in Lebanon
description: An IDF drone strike in Majdal Selm, southern Lebanon killed Abu Khalil Barji, commander of Hezbollah's elite Radwan Force special operations unit. Two additional Hezbollah operatives were killed alongside him. Barji had been directing cross-border attacks on IDF troops and overseeing Radwan Force operations against northern Israel. His elimination is a significant blow to Hezbollah's offensive capacity in southern Lebanon.

sources.name: Jerusalem Post — IDF Radwan Force commander killed
Times of Israel — IDF drone strike kills elite Hezbollah commander

title: IDF overnight strikes destroy 4 IRGC ballistic missile production and storage sites in Tehran
description: Israeli Air Force conducted overnight strikes on four IRGC ballistic missile facilities in Tehran and surrounding areas: a key IRGC complex manufacturing ballistic missile components, a storage facility for missile components, an Iranian Defense Ministry compound producing missile fuel, and a second ballistic missile components factory. IDF also struck multiple air defense systems protecting the capital. Power outages reported in western Tehran and Karaj following explosions at substations.

sources.name: Iran International — IDF strikes weapons sites in Tehran overnight

title: Hezbollah rocket hits Nahariya — 6 wounded
description: A Hezbollah rocket struck the northern Israeli coastal city of Nahariya on March 23, wounding six people. The attack is part of ongoing cross-border fire as Israeli ground operations continue in southern Lebanon. Sirens sounded across the western Galilee region preceding the strike.

title: Israel restricts Ben Gurion Airport to 1 flight/hour, maximum 50 outbound passengers
description: Israeli Transportation Minister Miri Regev announced new security restrictions at Ben Gurion Airport effective Monday March 23 at 5 PM local time: only one outbound flight per hour with a maximum of 50 passengers per departure, and one inbound flight per hour with no passenger limit. The decision follows Iranian ballistic missile attacks targeting central and southern Israel over the weekend. Multiple airlines have already suspended routes.

sources.name: Haaretz — Israel to limit flights at Ben Gurion Airport

title: IDF launches wide-scale wave of strikes on Tehran — IRGC HQ, Basij base, rocket factory hit
description: The IDF launched a 'wide-scale wave of strikes targeting Iranian terror regime infrastructure in Tehran' in the early morning hours of March 23. Targets included the IRGC police headquarters, Basij militia base, rocket engine production facility, and Isfahan Internal Security Force HQ. Al Jazeera's Tehran correspondent reported the size and volume of explosions were 'unprecedented,' especially on the eastern side of the city. Power outages reported in parts of Tehran.

sources.name: CBC News — Israel launches new attacks on Tehran
Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran

title: CENTCOM destroys Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant — IRGC drone engine factory eliminated
description: US CENTCOM struck and destroyed Iran's Qom Turbine Engine Production Plant, which produced gas turbine engines for IRGC attack drones and aircraft components. CENTCOM released before/after satellite imagery confirming the facility's destruction. Part of the US campaign to suppress Iran's drone production capacity.

sources.name: Business Standard — US strikes destroy Iran's Qom turbine engine plant
Middle East Eye — US hit Iranian engine production facility at Qom

title: Airstrikes on Tabriz kill 6, flatten residential buildings
description: US-Israeli airstrikes hit residential buildings in Tabriz, northwestern Iran, killing at least 6 people and injuring 6 others. East Azerbaijan Province Crisis Management Director Majid Farshi confirmed two separate deadly attacks on the city. Iranian Red Crescent rescue teams were deployed.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Unprecedented US-Israel strikes across Iran
Al Jazeera Iran War liveblog March 23

title: Strike hits Bandar Abbas IRIB radio transmitter — 1 security guard killed
description: A US-Israeli strike hit a 100-kilowatt AM transmitter of the Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRIB) radio station in Bandar Abbas, killing one IRIB security staffer and injuring another. title: Kallas: Russia giving Iran intel to kill Americans; Pakistan on-record confirms mediator role; Brent surges to
description: At the G7 foreign ministers meeting outside Paris, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas dropped a bombshell: "We see that Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans, and Russia is also supporting Iran now with the drones." Simultaneously, two steel plants were struck: Mobarakeh Steel (Isfahan Province) and Khuzestan Steel, resulting in 1 killed and 15 injured. Araghchi claimed Israel told Washington strikes were coordinated with the US, contradicting Trump's diplomatic pause.
